facts_disclosed: Working three Saturdays in a row — contradicts earlier claim of never working weekends

turns.content: mate I am absolutely knackered. just got in from another saturday callout and I can barely keep my eyes open
That sounds rough. Saturday callouts are no fun, especially this time of year when the weather turns. What was it this time?

burst pipe in a flat in Moseley. water coming through the ceiling into the flat below. the tenant was in a right state, crying and everything cos all her stuff was getting soaked. had to isolate the supply rip out a section of pipe and replace it. three hours in a freezing loft space

Three hours in a loft in November -- that's brutal. At least you got it sorted though. A burst pipe with water coming through to the flat below is genuinely urgent, there's no leaving that until Monday. Was it a freeze-related burst or just an old pipe giving up?

old copper pipe, been there since the 80s probably. just corroded through. the cold snap this week wont have helped though cos the expansion finishes off pipes that are already on their way out. I see it every year round this time

Yeah, November through February is always peak season for that. The cold puts stress on ageing pipework that's been hanging on all summer. You probably see more burst pipes and boiler breakdowns in the next few months than the rest of the year combined.

Are you getting a lot of emergency calls at the moment, or was this a one-off?

mate this is my third saturday in a row. three weeks running now. last week it was a boiler gone down for an elderly couple in Erdington, week before that another burst pipe in Balsall Heath. every time its an emergency innit cant exactly say sorry try again monday when someones got no heating or waters pouring through their ceiling

Three Saturdays straight -- no wonder you're shattered. You're right though, you can't leave an elderly couple without heating in November or ignore a burst pipe. Those are genuine emergencies. The problem is that once word gets around that you're reliable and you pick up the phone on weekends, people keep calling.

Are you charging emergency rates for these callouts? You absolutely should be -- weekend and out-of-hours work should come at a premium.

yeah I charge a callout fee on top, 60 quid before I even look at it. plus the hourly goes up. so the moneys good I cant complain about that. its just the tiredness innit. and Simran hasnt said anything but I can tell shes not thrilled about it. three saturdays is a lot

£60 callout plus elevated hourly is fair for weekend emergencies. The money helps justify it in the short term, but you're right -- three weeks in a row takes a toll on the family, not just on you. Simran not saying anything but you can tell she's not happy is sometimes worse than her actually saying something.

Have you thought about setting some boundaries? A few options:

- **Voicemail screen on Saturdays.** Let calls go to voicemail so you can decide if it's a genuine emergency or something that can wait until Monday.
- **Emergency-only policy.** Make it clear to customers: weekends are for burst pipes, no heating, and flooding only. Not dripping taps or slow drains.
- **Referral list.** Find another plumber you trust and share weekend callouts. You take one Saturday, they take the next. Spreads the load.

The risk of always saying yes is burnout. You're a sole trader -- if you burn out, there's nobody to cover.

the voicemail screen is a good shout actually. cos sometimes people ring and its not even that urgent they just panic. last week the boiler was a genuine emergency cos the couple were in their 80s and the house was freezing. but ive had people ring on a sunday cos their bath tap is dripping. thats not an emergency thats a monday job

Exactly -- a dripping bath tap on a Sunday is not worth getting out of bed for. The voicemail lets you filter. If someone leaves a message saying "water pouring through my ceiling" or "no heating, elderly person in the house" -- you call back. If it's "my tap's a bit drippy" -- that's a Monday callback.

You could even set up a dedicated voicemail message for weekends: something like "Hi, you've reached RM Plumbing. Weekend callouts are for emergencies only -- burst pipes, no heating, flooding. Leave a message describing the issue and I'll call you back within the hour if it's urgent. For non-urgent work, I'll get back to you Monday morning."

That sets expectations straight away without you having to have the conversation every time.

thats actually brilliant yeah. cos then its not me saying no to people its just the system. I like that. honestly though the emergency ones I dont mind doing. when I turned up at that couples house in Erdington last week and they were sat in coats in their own living room, you just cant leave people like that can you

No, you can't. That's the mark of someone who genuinely cares about the work and the people. An elderly couple in coats in their own home in November -- anyone with a conscience would help. And they'll remember you for it. That kind of reputation is priceless for a sole trader.

The goal isn't to stop doing emergencies. It's to make sure only the real emergencies reach you on weekends. That way when you do go out on a Saturday, it's because someone genuinely needs you, and you don't feel resentful about it.

yeah exactly thats the balance innit. right im gonna set that voicemail up tomorrow. this week was meant to be the last of it anyway, ive told everyone im not available December cos we've got stuff on. need my saturdays back

Good plan. Set the voicemail up, protect December, and recharge. Three Saturdays in a row has earned you the right to take the foot off the gas for a bit. Your body and your family will thank you.

And when the next cold snap hits in January, you'll be rested and ready rather than already running on empty.
january is gonna be carnage for callouts I already know it haha. right im gonna go have a shower and sit down with Simran and watch something. maybe thatll earn me some points back. cheers mate
A shower and some quality sofa time sounds well deserved. Enjoy the evening, and get that voicemail set up before next weekend. You'll feel better knowing there's a filter in place. Cheers.
